title: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu

seo.title: Dubu Jorim Recipe: Spicy Korean Braised Tofu (Vegetarian)
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Dubu Jorim, a spicy Korean braised tofu dish. This easy vegetarian recipe is packed with flavor and perfect for a healthy meal. Get the full recipe now!
seo.h1: Dubu Jorim: Spicy Korean Braised Tofu (Vegetarian)
h2Headings: Ingredients for Spicy Korean Braised Tofu
How to Make Dubu Jorim (Step-by-Step Guide)
Tips for Perfect Spicy Braised Tofu
Frequently Asked Questions About Dubu Jorim
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of Dubu Jorim, spicy Korean braised tofu, garnished with green onions and sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this flavorful and healthy Dubu Jorim, a classic spicy Korean braised tofu dish perfect for any meal!

description: Dubu Jorim is a beloved Korean side dish featuring firm tofu braised in a savory, spicy, and slightly sweet sauce. This vegetarian delight offers a wonderful texture contrast and bursts with authentic Korean flavors. It's a comforting and healthy option perfect alongside rice or as part of a larger meal.

ingredients.notes: pressed and sliced into 1/2-inch thick rectangles

instructions.title: Prepare the Tofu
instructions.text: Press the tofu block for at least 30 minutes to remove excess water. You can do this by wrapping it in paper towels or a clean cloth and placing a heavy object on top. Once pressed, slice the tofu into 1/2-inch thick, rectangular pieces.

instructions.title: Make the Braising Sauce
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the soy sauce, gochugaru, minced garlic, minced ginger, sesame oil, sugar or maple syrup, and water or vegetable broth. Whisk well until all ingredients are thoroughly mixed and the sugar has dissolved. Set aside.

instructions.title: Pan-Fry the Tofu
instructions.text: Heat cooking oil in a large non-stick pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Carefully place the tofu slices in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Fry for 3-4 minutes per side, or until golden brown and slightly crispy. Remove the tofu from the pan and set aside on a plate.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Pour the prepared braising sauce into the same pan. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally. Allow it to cook for 2-3 minutes until it starts to thicken slightly.

instructions.title: Braise the Tofu
instructions.text: Carefully return the pan-fried tofu slices to the simmering sauce, arranging them in a single layer. Spoon the sauce over the tofu to ensure each piece is coated. Let the tofu braise in the sauce for 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until the sauce has reduced and thickened to your desired consistency and the tofu has absorbed the flavors.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the Dubu Jorim is ready, transfer it to a serving dish. Garnish generously with sliced green onions and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds. Serve immediately with a bowl of warm rice and other Korean side dishes.

tips: Always press your tofu thoroughly to achieve a firmer texture and allow it to absorb the braising sauce more effectively.
For an extra layer of flavor, consider adding a few slices of onion or a handful of shiitake mushrooms to the braising sauce along with the tofu.
Dubu Jorim tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
article: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu is a beloved Korean Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
